title: Artificial Intelligence and Public Standards: government response to report
description: The government's response to the Committee's 2020 report Artificial Intelligence and Public Standards
summary: The British government has issued a response to a report on artificial intelligence and public standards. The response outlines their strategy and goals for implementing and regulating AI in accordance with the report's recommendations.

title: Report and impact assessment on Copyright and Artificial Intelligence
description: A report and impact assessment on the use of copyright works in the development of artificial intelligence (AI) systems.
summary: This document assesses the impact of copyright laws on the use of copyrighted materials in artificial intelligence development. It reports on the challenges and consequences of copyright infringement in the creation of AI systems.

title: Generative artificial intelligence (AI) in education
description: The Department for Education’s (DfE) position on the use of generative AI in the education sector.
summary: The Department for Education outlines its stance on using generative artificial intelligence in educational settings, providing guidelines and policies for its application. This policy aims to ensure the safe and effective use of generative AI in education.

title: Transport artificial intelligence action plan
description: Sets out how DfT will work with the transport sector to exploit the opportunities from AI while managing potential risks.
summary: The Transport artificial intelligence action plan outlines a strategy for the Department of Transport to use artificial intelligence in the transport sector, aiming to maximize its benefits while minimizing risks. This plan will involve collaboration with the transport sector to leverage AI technology and ensure its safe and effective use.

title: Copyright and artificial intelligence: progress report
description: A progress report on the use of copyright works in the development of AI systems, under Section 137 of the Data (Use and Access) Act.
summary: This progress report examines how copyright works are being used in the development of artificial intelligence (AI) systems. The report specifically focuses on the intersection of copyright and AI development under a particular section of the Data (Use and Access) Act.

title: The regulation of artificial intelligence as a medical device: government response to the RHC
description: Government response to the recommendations made by the Regulatory Horizons Council (RHC) on the regulation of AI as a medical device.
summary: The government has responded to recommendations for regulating AI as a medical device, aiming to ensure its safe and effective use in healthcare. The goal is to create a regulatory framework that balances innovation with patient safety and medical device standards.

title: ​​Ofqual’s approach to regulating the use of artificial intelligence in the qualifications sector​
description: Published in response to a request from the Secretary of State for Education and the Secretary of State for Science, Innovation and Technology.
summary: Ofqual regulates the use of artificial intelligence in the qualifications sector to ensure standards are maintained and fairness is upheld. They approach this regulation in response to requests from the Education and Science Ministers to address potential concerns.

title: Government response to the House of Lords Select Committee on Artificial Intelligence
description: Government response to the Lords Select Committee on Artificial Intelligence report ‘AI in the UK: No Room for Complacency’.
summary: The UK government is responding to a report about Artificial Intelligence, specifically addressing concerns from the House of Lords Select Committee on A.I. The response aims to outline the government's actions regarding A.I. in the UK following the "No Room for Complacency" report.

title: AI and Public Standards – Terms of Reference
description: Terms of Reference and FAQs for the Committee's review into artificial intelligence and public standards.
summary: This document outlines the terms and guidelines for a committee reviewing the impact of artificial intelligence on public standards. It is intended to provide clarity and information for the committee's review process.

title: CSPL submission to Science and Technology Committee inquiry on Governance of AI
description: CSPL has submitted written evidence to the Science and Technology Committee's inquiry on Governance of Artificial Intelligence.
summary: The UK's Committee on Standards in Public Life (CSPL) has provided written evidence to a government inquiry on how to regulate Artificial Intelligence. The document explores potential policies for governing AI to ensure its safe and ethical use in the UK.
